# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

This is an educational, open-source project. 
I/We are committed to maintaining a welcoming environment for everyone, regardless of background, experience level, or academic affiliation.

## Expected Behaviour

- Be respectful and constructive in issues, pull requests, and discussions.
- Provide helpful, specific feedback when reviewing code.
- Credit external sources (papers, blog posts, reference implementations) in your contributions.
- Welcome questions — this project is meant to be a learning resource.

## Unacceptable Behaviour

- Personal attacks, insults, or harassment.
- Dismissing contributions or questions because they are from beginners.
- Deliberately misleading others about algorithmic correctness or complexity.

## Enforcement

Issues that violate this policy can be reported to me via the contact on the GitHub profile. Violations will result in comments being removed and, in serious cases, the contributor being blocked.

---

*This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/).*
